primary_accounting_issue: Which IFRS hedge accounting models can be documented in consolidated financial statements for foreign exchange risk arising from an intragroup dividend receivable.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern which items may be designated as hedged items in hedge accounting and specifically address intragroup items in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: This Interpretation clarifies the distinct hedge accounting model for a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation and how the hedging instrument is assessed in a group context.
These paragraphs help distinguish hedge-designated derivatives from derivatives used without formal designation by addressing profit or loss classification consequences.

peripheral_authority.reason: These sections address scope and presentation of financial instruments and dividends, not the identification of hedge accounting models for intragroup FX risk.
These paragraphs deal with disclosure relief and dividend disclosures, not hedge accounting approach identification.
This Interpretation concerns non-cash distributions to owners, not hedge accounting for FX on intragroup dividend receivables.
This Interpretation addresses members’ shares in co-operatives and related classification issues, not hedging of intragroup dividend receivables.
These paragraphs concern cash flow statement classification and financing-liability reconciliations, not hedge accounting model identification.
This Interpretation addresses tax consequences of changes in tax status, which is unrelated to the hedge accounting issue.
These are disclosure requirements about subsidiaries, restrictions and dividends, not hedge accounting models.
IAS 37 deals with provisions and contingencies, which is not relevant to identifying hedge accounting approaches here.
This Interpretation addresses debt-for-equity extinguishments, not hedging of foreign exchange risk.
These sections address scope, own-use contracts and measurement of financial instruments generally, but do not directly identify the hedge accounting models relevant to the question.

treatment_families.family: General IFRS hedge accounting models available for foreign exchange risk when an eligible hedged item exists in consolidated financial statements
Hedge accounting for foreign currency exposure linked to a foreign operation at group level

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS hedge accounting includes fair value hedging as a top-level model for eligible foreign exchange exposures once a qualifying hedged item exists in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS hedge accounting includes cash flow hedging as a top-level model, including for eligible highly probable forecast transactions and other qualifying FX exposures.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 identifies hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model relevant to group foreign currency exposures.
